# Windows MSIX Strategy

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostlocalipcdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host local IPC](../local-ipc.md)

`msix-sideload` and `msix-store` share local runtime responsibilities, but review and update boundaries differ.

| Channel | Runtime shape | Startup/background rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `msix-sideload` | Signed MSIX with Store App plus local helper/broker under package identity | Explicit `background-helper` consent; Windows service install is disabled by default |
| `msix-store` | Microsoft Store packaged desktop app plus full-trust helper/tray process | Store-managed updates; background helper requires consent and Store review approval |

1. Provider CLIs are not included in MSIX packages.
2. `ExternalToolRegistry` preserves user-selected, env-override, and auto-detected provenance only.
3. Store App to helper control surface is Windows named pipe only; external TCP listener is forbidden.
4. App data and daemon state use package local data roots; user profile fallback is rejected.
5. Workspace access reaches C6 only when a Windows folder-picker grant and workspace consent are both active.
6. `msix-store` must support review/demo mode without provider installation.

`ResolveHelperRuntimePlan` computes MSIX roles and named-pipe local IPC, but does not spawn provider processes, open named pipes, or install Windows services.
